Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Penante, Brenda, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise (sunset) self-energy integral with three equal internal masses in $d=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. The paper expresses the $d=2$ part in terms of elliptic multiple polylogarithms and rewrites it as iterated integrals of Eisenstein series for $\Gamma(6)$ using the elliptic symbol calculus developed in the paper.

Two-loop sunrise graph with three equal internal masses in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. The geometry is a single elliptic curve. Epsilon-factorised DE obtained via semi-simple part of period matrix; no extra functions $G_i$ needed. The GM connection involves the holomorphic period $\varpi_0$ but not its derivative.

Equal-mass sunrise (banana) integral $S_{111}(x)$ in two space-time dimensions with three equal-mass propagators and one external momentum $p$, where $x=p^2/m^2$. The result is expressed as iterated integrals of modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$, evaluated numerically to arbitrary precision via $\bar{q}$-expansion in GiNaC. The analytical formula involves the Clausen function $\operatorname{Cl}_2(\pi/3)$ and a depth-2 iterated integral of an Eisenstein series of weight 3. Numerical values are given for $x=9.01$ (slow convergence near threshold $p^2=9m^2$) and $x=0.01$ (fast convergence). The underlying analytical result in terms of modular forms is from Adams-Weinzierl (arXiv:1704.08895).
Records.location: Eq. in Section 5.3 (line after 'Let us consider the equal-mass sunrise integral'), Section 5.3

Records.description: Two-loop equal-mass banana (sunrise) integral in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. Related to an elliptic curve (Calabi-Yau 1-fold). Picard-Fuchs operator is second order with singular points at $x \in \{0,1,9,\infty\}$. Three master integrals. The $\varepsilon$-factorised differential equation was previously given in arXiv:1802.05020. The differential one-forms are modular forms.

Equal-mass sunrise integral ($m_1=m_2=m_3=m$) with elliptic highest sector. 3 master integrals: one double-tadpole and two in the highest sector. Epsilon-factorized differential equations obtained via period-matrix diagonalization with complete elliptic integrals $K$ and $E$.

Two-loop sunset (sunrise) self-energy with three equal masses in $D=2$. Result is the elliptic dilogarithm $E_{\text{sunset}}(q)$ plus periods of the elliptic curve. Satisfies Picard-Fuchs ODE in $t=K^2/m^2$.

Records.authors: Bezuglov, M.A., Onishchenko, A.I., Veretin, O.L.
Records.description: Two-loop sunset (sunrise) diagram with three equal internal masses $m=1$ in $d=4-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. Results expressed as iterated integrals with algebraic kernels via Feynman parametrization and differential equations in $\varepsilon$-form. 3 master integrals computed through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^3)$.
Records.familyName: sunrise equal mass (4d) / sunrise equal mass (2d)

Records.description: Equal-mass two-loop sunrise (sunset/London-transport) integral with $q_1=k_1$, $q_2=k_2-k_1$, $q_3=-k_2-p$; three master integrals $\{I_{110}, I_{111}, I_{211}\}$. Pre-canonical DE has singular points at $x \in \{0,-1,-9\}$; in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ the family is solved in $\varepsilon$-form via the elliptic curve $E^{\text{cut}}$, modular parameter $\tau$, and iterated integrals of Eisenstein-type modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$. Closed-form $\bar{q}$-expansion in eq. sunrise_final_qbar_expansion. Canonical example of an elliptic Feynman integral.
Records.familyName: equal-mass two-loop sunrise I_{nu1 nu2 nu3}
Records.location: Chapter 'Iterated integrals', Sec. 'Differential equations', Example 4 (line ~1386, eq. dgl_equal_mass_sunrise); Chapter 'Elliptics', Sec. 'Feynman integrals depending on one kinematic variable', eq. def_equal_mass_sunrise (line ~2891) and full solution lines ~2880-3700

Records.authors: Frellesvig, Hjalte, Gasparotto, Federico, Laporta, Stefano, Mandal, Manoj K., Mastrolia, Pierpaolo, Mattiazzi, Luca, Mizera, Sebastian
Records.description: Equal-mass massive two-loop sunrise (Broadhurst hep-ph/9304249, Laporta hep-ph/0410259) with three propagators of mass $m=1$ and one ISP $z=k_2^2$ in the variable $x=\sqrt{s}$. Intersection-number reduction in the dlog basis yields 3 masters, reduced to 2 independent ones via a sunrise symmetry relation between dotted integrals. A second-order ODE is derived for the canonical-like form, and at $d=2$ the homogeneous solutions are explicit complete elliptic integrals $K$ and $E$ with modulus $\chi(x)=16 x/((x-1)^3(x+3))$, exhibiting the elliptic structure of this graph.

Two-loop equal-mass sunrise (banana) self-energy integral with three massive propagators (all mass $m$) and one external momentum $p$. Worked in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions as the canonical single-scale example of a Feynman integral evaluating to iterated integrals of modular forms. The maximal cut yields an elliptic curve $E_x$ (Eq. E_73_maxcut) with modulus $k^2$ built from roots $z_1=-4$, $z_2=-(1+\sqrt{x})^2$, $z_3=-(1-\sqrt{x})^2$, $z_4=0$ and $x=p^2/m^2$. A change of variable $x \to \tau$ (hauptmodul $x = 9 \eta(6\tau)^8 \eta(\tau)^4 / (\eta(2\tau)^8 \eta(3\tau)^4)$) together with a rescaling of the sunrise master integral by $\pi/\psi_1$ brings the $3 \times 3$ differential equation for (tadpole $I_0$, sunrise $I_1$, its $\tau$-derivative $I_2$) into $\varepsilon$-form with integration kernels $f_2, f_3, f_4$ that are modular forms of $\Gamma_1(6)$ of weights 2, 3, 4. These modular forms are expressed as polynomials in weight-1 generalized Eisenstein series $e_1 = E_1(\tau;\chi_0,\chi_1)$ and $e_2 = E_1(2\tau;\chi_0,\chi_1)$. The solution to all orders in $\varepsilon$ is given by iterated integrals of modular forms, with the $q$-expansion providing efficient numerical evaluation.
Records.location: Section 'Feynman integrals evaluating to iterated integrals of modular forms', Eqs. (maxcut)-(integration_kernels); Fig. 3 (figex1)

Records.description: Two-loop sunset with all three internal propagators massive. Possesses three master integrals, labelled (1), (2), (3) in the paper with red/blue/green colours. The diagrammatic coaction reproduces the pairing structure seen in the one-mass and two-mass cases and acquires deformation terms with coefficient 1 (differing from the one-loop value $1/2$), formed by contracting a propagator inside a loop that has an even number of propagators.
Records.familyName: Two-loop fully-massive sunset (three internal masses) / This family does not exist in the paper. The paper discusses only a one-mass sunset (Section 3-4) and a two-mass sunset (Section 4). The three-master basis labeled red/blue/green is still for the two-mass sunset, not a three-mass sunset. Family [2] should be deleted.
Records.location: Section 4, diagrammatic coaction with a three-master basis labelled red/blue/green

Records.description: 2-loop equal-mass sunrise with 3 masters. Eps-form DE via elliptic basis and coordinate change $\tau=\psi_2/\psi_1$, yielding modular forms of $\Gamma_1(6)$ weights 2,3,4. Iterated integrals of modular forms to all orders in $\varepsilon$.
Equal-mass two-loop sunrise with one off-shell external leg. Monodromy generators in $GL(3,\mathbb{Z}[z,1/z])$.
Two-loop equal-mass sunset in $D=4-2\varepsilon$ via dimension-raising from $D=2$. Finite part in GPLs+elliptic.
Equal-mass sunrise in $d=2-2\varepsilon$ demonstrating parameter-space method at two loops. Second-order elliptic ODE with singular points at $r=1$ and $r=9$.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Matthes, Nils
Records.description: Two-loop equal-mass sunrise self-energy integral. The master integrals satisfy a $2 \times 2$ system of first-order differential equations which, after modular parametrisation with $\Gamma_1(6)$, can be brought to canonical form and solved to all orders in $\varepsilon$ in terms of iterated integrals of holomorphic modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$. Reproduces results of Adams et al.

2-loop equal-mass sunrise (banana) integral in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. 3 massive propagators. Symbol and coaction constructed via unipotent differential equations with Frobenius basis. Elliptic curve geometry.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise integral with three equal massive propagators in $d=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. Master integrals $S_{111}$ and $S_{211}$ computed analytically in terms of elliptic multiple polylogarithms (eMPLs) by direct Feynman parameter integration. $S_{110}$ is a product of one-loop integrals. Results given at $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$ and $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^1)$ for $S_{111}$, $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$ for $S_{211}$; algorithm extends to all orders in $\varepsilon$.

Records.authors: Adams, Luise, Bogner, Christian, Weinzierl, Stefan
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise with equal masses in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. All-order algorithm for $\varepsilon$ expansion using elliptic ELi functions parametrized by the nome $q$.

Two-loop equal-mass sunrise self-energy integral with three massive propagators in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Expressed to all orders in $\varepsilon$ as iterated integrals of modular forms of $\Gamma_1(6)$. Alphabet: $\{1, f_2, f_3, f_4\}$. Three master integrals: $S_{110}, S_{111}, S_{211}$.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Penante, Brenda, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop equal-mass sunrise integral family in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ with three master integrals computed in terms of elliptic multiple polylogarithms (pure eMPLs) of uniform transcendental weight two.

Two-loop equal-mass sunrise in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Picard-Fuchs operator is 2nd order, singular at $z=0,1/9,1,\infty$. $D=2$ result as iterated Eisenstein series for $\Gamma_1(6)$. Numerical to $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Marzucca, Robin, Penante, Brenda, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise graph with three equal non-zero internal masses, reviewed to establish connection with the banana family. 3 master integrals (2 non-trivial + 1 tadpole). Results in $d=2$ as iterated integrals of modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$.
Two-loop equal-mass sunrise in $d=2$ via parametric IBP. Yields Picard-Fuchs equation with elliptic singularities at $r=0,1,9$. Methodological example.

Records.authors: Gerlach, Marvin, Mishima, Go, Steinhauser, Matthias
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise (banana) integral with all three massive propagators (same mass $m$), external momentum $p$ ($p^2=m^2$). Result expanded through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$, involving $\zeta_3$, $\pi^2$, and $\pi^2 \log(2)$. Needed for the scattering channel of the NRQCD matching.

Two-loop equal-mass sunrise with three massive propagators and one external leg, $p_1^2=x m^2$. Three master integrals; canonical $\varepsilon$-form basis built from periods $\omega_1,\omega_2$ of the elliptic curve from the maximal cut. After $x \to \tau$ the differential equation becomes $dJ=-\varepsilon A J$ with $A$ in modular forms $\eta_k(\tau)$ of $\Gamma(6)$. Results are iterated integrals of modular forms through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$; boundary: $\operatorname{Cl}_2(2\pi/3)$.
On-shell equal-mass sunrise with $p^2=M^2$; master integral for two-loop top-quark self-energy in SMEFT. Closed form from literature.
Equal-mass limit of unequal-mass sunrise to illustrate degenerate-mass subtleties in Baikov/intersection reductions.

Two-loop equal-mass sunrise (sunset) self-energy with three internal massive propagators and one external momentum $p_1$ with $s=-p_1^2$. The paper presents the Feynman parametric representation, applies partial quadratization to the second Symanzik polynomial, and derives a closed-form Mellin-Barnes/Fox H-function representation studied near the pseudo- ($\lambda=1$) and normal ($\lambda=3$) thresholds.

Review of the equal-mass two-loop sunrise in $d=2-2\varepsilon$: all-order $\varepsilon$ expansion in terms of iterated integrals of modular forms on a congruence subgroup of $SL_2(\mathbb{Z})$ with alphabet $\{1, f_2, f_3, f_4\}$. Elliptic curve from the second Symanzik; $\varepsilon$-form DE after a non-algebraic change of variable and extracting $\psi_1/\pi$. Results translate to $d=4-2\varepsilon$ via dimensional-shift relations.

Proceedings review illustrating the threefold representation of elliptic multiple polylogarithms (eMPLs $E_4$, $\tilde{\Gamma}$ on the torus, iterated integrals of Eisenstein series for $\Gamma(6)$) on the example of the equal-mass two-loop sunrise. The auxiliary scalar function $I(u)$ in Eq. (3.4) is expressed in closed form in each of the three representations. No IBP or master basis is constructed here; the paper reviews results of Remiddi-Tancredi (1709.03622).
Records.location: Section 3, Eqs. (3.1)-(3.3); example integral I(u) in Eq. (3.4)

Records.description: Equal-mass two-loop sunrise self-energy with three massive propagators of common mass $m$, evaluated directly in $d=4$ using an alternative perturbative regularization scheme. Divergent pieces are isolated via a propagator-expansion identity; remaining finite Feynman-parameter integrals are computed analytically, yielding a closed-form expression in terms of elliptic multiple polylogarithms on the elliptic curve $y^2 = x(1-x)(p^2 x (1-x) - m^2)$. Numerical cross-check against the literature is provided.
Records.location: Section 3, Eqs. (3.1)-(3.3); analytic result Section 5; numerical check Section 6

Records.authors: Ananthanarayan, Balasubramanian, Banik, Sumit, Bernard, Véronique, Friot, Samuel, Ghosh, Shayan, Meißner, Ulf-G.
Records.description: Sunset master integral $H_{1,1,1}(m,m,m;m^2)$ with a single mass scale. On-shell pseudothreshold configuration with equal masses on all three propagators and $p^2=m^2$. Result is rational combination of $\pi^2$ at $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$. Full $\varepsilon$-dependent result via Mellin-Barnes representation. Context: chiral perturbation theory meson masses.

Two-loop massive sunrise (sunset) diagram with three equal-mass internal propagators (mass $m_2$) and two equal-mass external legs (mass $m_1$). Defined with 3 propagators $z_1, z_2, z_3$ plus 2 irreducible scalar products $z_4 = l_1^2$ and $z_5 = (l_2+p_1)^2$. The Baikov twist is not purely quadratic (involves two loop momenta). The $C$-matrix is block diagonal (not fully diagonal): the first three master integrals yield a $3 \times 3$ diagonal block, while the remaining three form a $3 \times 3$ off-diagonal block involving 'derivative delta forms'. This is the only two-loop example in the paper and demonstrates partial success of the diagonal basis prescription beyond one-loop.

Banana family, banana_L2_equal-mass-internal. Banana (sunrise) graph: two vertices joined by L+1 edges, L loops, equal internal masses. Evaluates to Calabi-Yau (L-1)-fold periods; GKZ systems give the all-L structure; eps-factorized at all L.
